name: Tomato-Basil Soup (Weir)
recipeIngredient: 8 sprigs fresh basil, tied together with kitchen string -- plus
1/2 cup loosely packed fresh basil leaves, cut into thin strips
8 large ripe tomatoes, peeled, seeded and chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a large soup pot over medium-high heat.
2. Add the onion and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft, about 7 minutes.
3. Add the basil sprigs, tomatoes, chicken stock and 1 cup water to the pot.
4. Bring to a boil over high heat.
5. Reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer until the liquid is reduced by one-quarter, about 20 minutes.
6. Cool for 10 minutes.
7. Remove the basil and discard it.
8. In a blender, puree the soup in several batches until smooth, 2-3 minutes per batch.
9. Strain into a clean pot and bring to a simmer over medium heat.
10. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
11. To serve, ladle the soup into bowls and garnish with the basil leaves.
Notes: This soup can be made up to a day in advance and stored in the refrigerator until ready to serve. Reheat over medium heat until hot, about 5 minutes.
